warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.foodCategory: seasonings and condiments, snack foods
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the online database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.foodCategory: seasonings and condiments, snack foods
rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.foodCategory: seasonings and condiments
rules.limit: Adherence to Code of Practice required for prevention and reduction
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices CCCF 2017

rules.foodCategory: Not specified
rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The EU Additives Database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.foodCategory: Not specified
rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, and process hygiene criteria for foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.foodCategory: Processed foods, seasoning, snack food

rules.foodCategory: Food & Beverages (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)

rules.notes: These substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74, 182, 184, 181) or evaluated by FEMA/JECFA for flavorings.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*. Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184). Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: These nine major food allergens must be specifically labeled on packaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.foodCategory: Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool | FDA
rules.notes: This is a guidance level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.foodCategory: Foods (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool | FDA
rules.notes: This is an action level for total aflatoxins in general foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.foodCategory: Human food (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.parameter: Various chemical contaminants (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool | FDA
rules.notes: Contaminant levels, including tolerances, action levels, and guidance levels, are published in various guidance documents and regulations and consolidated in the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.foodCategory: Foods (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Foods labeled "gluten-free" must meet a defined standard for gluten content, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling, which helps consumers, especially those living with celiac disease, be confident that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content.

rules.foodCategory: Packaged foods and beverages (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Advisory statements are not required by law and should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices. They must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact... FDA guidance and regulations for the food industry states that advisory statements should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices and must be truthful and not misleading.

rules.foodCategory: Human food (establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold, including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Establishments must implement written procedures to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.foodCategory: Food and cosmetic products (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.foodCategory: Packaged foods and beverages (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the name of the ingredient.
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. Examples: “lecithin (soy),” “flour (wheat),” and “whey (milk)”

rules.foodCategory: Packaged foods and beverages (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.parameter: Declaration of specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.foodCategory: Products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.foodCategory: Foods (including seasonings, snack foods, processed foods)
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 ppm total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.foodCategory: Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: Specific permitted food additives and their maximum usage levels for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế

rules.foodCategory: Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: A list of mandatory allergens requiring declaration on labels for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế

rules.foodCategory: Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: Specific limits for chemical contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, pesticides) in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế

rules.foodCategory: Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: General labelling requirements for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, such as nutritional information, ingredient lists, and country of origin, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế

rules.foodCategory: Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế

rules.foodCategory: Processed Foods
rules.sourceTitle: Vietnam Government Document Portal
rules.notes: Specific limits or recommendations for sodium/salt content in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
rules.evidenceQuote: Lĩnh vực: Y tế - Sức khỏe; Cơ quan ban hành: Bộ Y tế
